title: SWIFT CSCF 6.1 ‚Äî malware protection across the SWIFT secure zone
exclusions: Does NOT substitute for the full regulatory control set; this UC provides one of several evidence streams and must be combined with policy, procedure, and supplemental UCs to demonstrate the complete clause.
evidence: Scheduled search output persisted to the compliance summary index with regulation=SWIFT CSP, clause=6.1, available as a saved search and exportable as a CSV evidence pack for the responsible officer.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Phase-C authored UC targeting SWIFT CSP 6.1 (Malware protection). Uses continuous, production-grade Splunk telemetry to provide auditor-facing evidence; assurance level set to 'contributing' pending SME review per Phase E of the regulation-coverage plan.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that SWIFT CSP 6.1 (Malware protection) is enforced ‚Äî Splunk UC-22.50.13: SWIFT CSCF 6.1 ‚Äî malware protection across the SWIFT secure zone.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.50.13' running on Endpoint detection and response (EDR), antivirus logs from the SWIFT secure zone, CSP attestation evidence., archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted ‚Äî SME review required.
compliance.smeCaveat: Phase-C draft: SME review required before uplifting assurance from 'contributing' to 'partial' or 'full'. Confirm that the SPL captures the specific regulator expectation and that the telemetry exists in the deployment.

dataSources: Endpoint detection and response (EDR), antivirus logs from the SWIFT secure zone, CSP attestation evidence.
spl:
index=swift_zone earliest=-24h
| search (sourcetype="av" OR sourcetype="edr")
| stats count AS events count(eval(signature_coverage_hours <= 24)) AS current_coverage count(eval(action="block")) AS blocks BY host, edr_vendor
| eval up_to_date=if(current_coverage=events, "yes", "no")
| where up_to_date="no" OR blocks > 0
description: Confirms every SWIFT-secure-zone host is running malware protection with current signatures, evidencing CSCF 6.1.
value: CSCF 6.1 is a mandatory control; continuous coverage checks produce annual KYC-SA attestation evidence on demand.
implementation: (1) Identify the SWIFT secure zone with a 'zone=swift' asset field; (2) route EDR logs through a dedicated source; (3) schedule daily. Indexes required: `index=swift_zone`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=av`, `sourcetype=edr`.
visualization: Table of flagged records for investigation; single-value KPI of coverage / compliance percentage; time chart of trend over the last 90 days; drill-down per responsible owner. Key fields: `blocks`, `current_coverage`, `edr_vendor`, `events`, `host`, `up_to_date`.

references.title: SWIFT Customer Security Control Framework (CSCF) v2025

knownFalsePositives: Tuning prerequisites: source field mappings must be in place before the UC will produce reliable metrics; early runs will commonly flag unmapped systems until onboarding is completed.
grandmaExplanation: We watch for confirms every swift-secure-zone host is running malware protection with current signatures, evidencing cscf 6.1. This matters because it catches problems that could lead to audit findings or compliance gaps.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform.
‚Ä¢ Ensure the following data sources are available: Endpoint detection and response (EDR), antivirus logs from the SWIFT secure zone, CSP attestation evidence..
‚Ä¢ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection
(1) Identify the SWIFT secure zone with a 'zone=swift' asset field; (2) route EDR logs through a dedicated source; (3) schedule daily.

Step 2 ‚Äî Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=swift_zone earliest=-24h
| search (sourcetype="av" OR sourcetype="edr")
| stats count AS events count(eval(signature_coverage_hours <= 24)) AS current_coverage count(eval(action="block")) AS blocks BY host, edr_vendor
| eval up_to_date=if(current_coverage=events, "yes", "no")
| where up_to_date="no" OR blocks > 0
```

Understanding this SPL

**SWIFT CSCF 6.1 ‚Äî malware protection across the SWIFT secure zone** ‚Äî CSCF 6.1 is a mandatory control; continuous coverage checks produce annual KYC-SA attestation evidence on demand.

Documented **Data sources**: Endpoint detection and response (EDR), antivirus logs from the SWIFT secure zone, CSP attestation evidence. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: swift_zone.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Scopes the data: index=swift_zone, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
‚Ä¢ Applies an explicit `search` filter to narrow the current result set.
‚Ä¢ `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by host, edr_vendor** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
‚Ä¢ `eval` defines or adjusts **up_to_date** ‚Äî often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
‚Ä¢ Filters the current rows with `where up_to_date="no" OR blocks > 0` ‚Äî typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.


Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table of flagged records for investigation; single-value KPI of coverage / compliance percentage; time chart of trend over the last 90 days; drill-down per responsible owner.
